title: Vertical Farming
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Vertical farming is (0) an innovative agricultural method that involves growing crops in vertically stacked layers, often in controlled indoor environments. This approach could revolutionise food production, particularly in urban areas where space is limited.||Unlike traditional farming, vertical farms use hydroponics or aeroponics, (9) which means crops are grown without soil. Instead, plants receive nutrients directly through water solutions. LED lights replace sunlight, and climate control systems maintain optimal growing conditions year-round. This allows crops (10) to be harvested multiple times per year regardless of season or weather.||Vertical farming offers several advantages (11) over conventional agriculture. It uses up to 95% less water and requires no pesticides since the controlled environment prevents pest infestations. Food can be grown efficiently and (12) without the need for long-distance transportation, reducing costs and emissions. The predictable yields and faster growing cycles (13) make vertical farming particularly attractive for growing leafy greens and herbs.||However, there are challenges. The technology requires significant initial investment, and operating costs can (14) be high due to energy consumption for lighting and climate control. Critics argue that until renewable energy becomes cheaper, vertical farming may/might/could (15) not be environmentally sustainable. Despite these concerns, vertical farming is expanding rapidly, (16) with facilities opening in cities worldwide as technology improves and costs decrease.

example.text: Vertical farming is (0) innovative agricultural method.

questions.explanation: Relative pronoun referring to hydroponics or aeroponics.
Expression 'allows crops to be harvested' with a passive infinitive.
Expression 'advantages over' indicates comparison with traditional agriculture.
Preposition meaning 'in the absence of', used to indicate that something is not needed.
Simple present: 'cycles make' with a plural subject.
Estructura modal: 'can be high' expresa posibilidad.
Negation in modal structure: 'may/might/could not be sustainable'.
Preposition 'with' introduces additional information about facilities.
